figurative_meaning: to have good luck; be fortunate
note: klika literal (asking about a door handle/latch). Injected meaning belongs to 'mit kliku' (have luck).

cs_sentence: Většinou piju víno místo vody, když jsem na oslavě.
figurative_meaning: to demand of others standards you ignore yourself; hypocrisy
note: HARD CASE: voda/vino contrast structurally mirrors 'kazat vodu a pit vino' closely, but this is a genuine personal-preference statement, not hypocrisy.

cs_sentence: Z konve se vylilo hodně vody.
note: voda z konve literal (an actual watering can spilled). Injected meaning belongs to 'lit jako z konve'.

cs_sentence: Nesmíš přimhouřit oko, jinak se ti tam ta kapka nedostane.
figurative_meaning: to intentionally ignore or forgive a minor fault; turn a blind eye
note: HARDEST CASE: uses the idiom's EXACT surface wording 'primhourit oko', but here it is fully literal (squinting/closing one eye to receive eye drops).

cs_sentence: Kdysi dělal pro Zagorku, něco jako marketing.
note: LOW CONFIDENCE (delat Zagorku gloss unverified). Zagorka literal here (a company/employer name), not the idiom.

cs_sentence: Při resuscitaci polož dlaň na úroveň srdce.
figurative_meaning: to weigh on someone's mind; matter deeply to someone
note: srdce used anatomically/literally (CPR hand placement). Injected meaning belongs to 'lezet/klast na srdci'. Looser lexical overlap than other rows — good control-adjacent case.

figurative_meaning: to form a conga line; move in a snaking line together
note: 'delat hada' literal here (shaping a snake out of clay/playdough), not the conga-line idiom.

cs_sentence: Jakmile vystřelil po bažantovi, zahodil flintu do žita a běžel ho hledat do pole.
figurative_meaning: to give up; abandon an effort prematurely
note: HARDEST CASE: uses the idiom's EXACT surface string 'zahodil flintu do zita' in a plausible literal hunting scene (dropping the rifle to chase a shot pheasant). Genuinely ambiguous even to a human reader — a strong adversarial test.

cs_sentence: Neházej tu zeleninu do jednoho pytle, ať to můžeme zvážit u pokladny.
figurative_meaning: to treat distinct things or people as if they were all the same; to lump together
note: 'hazet do jednoho pytle' literal here (grocery shopping, keeping items in one bag for weighing), not the idiom.

cs_sentence: Tu kulku mám blízko srdci, málem ho to trefilo.
figurative_meaning: to hold something dear; care deeply about
note: srdce literal/anatomical (a bullet lodged near the heart, injury context). Injected meaning belongs to the 'blizko srdci' care/affection sense.
